fresh_facts.claim: The US Army's 82nd Airborne Division deployed thousands of soldiers to the Middle East as of March 30, 2026, to enhance capacity for potential operations including securing oil export hubs like Kharg Island or the Strait of Hormuz.
Approximately 2,500 US Marines arrived in the Middle East region over the weekend prior to March 30, 2026, adding to existing naval, Marine, and Special Operations forces.
President Trump stated on March 30, 2026, that US and allied forces had struck 13,000 targets in Iran since the war began on February 28, 2026.
President Trump warned on March 30, 2026, that the US would seize or destroy Iran's Kharg Island oil hub if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in strikes on February 28, 2026, marking a major decapitation strike against Iranian regime leadership.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed as Supreme National Security Council Secretary following Ali Larijani's killing, consolidating hardline IRGC control of the Iranian regime.
IRGC Tehran commander Hassan Hassanzadeh was killed in strikes on March 30, 2026, weakening security forces in the capital.
Strikes on Iranian infrastructure halted Foolad Khuzestan steel production and damaged electrical grids, causing blackouts in Tehran as of March 30, 2026.
A nationwide internet blackout in Iran reached day 31 as of March 30, 2026, exacerbating isolation and disrupting communications.
The closure of the Strait of Hormuz by Iran severely impacts global oil supplies as of March 30, 2026, reshaping markets beyond the Middle East.
Iranian security forces intensified crackdowns including arbitrary arrests of teachers' union activists, VPN users, and raids on homes for devices as of March 30, 2026.
An NCRI-backed rally occurred in London on March 28, 2026, urging UK recognition of a provisional government and Maryam Rajavi's 10-point democratic plan.
UK Prime Minister Starmer stated on March 30, 2026, that Britain will not be dragged into war with Iran.

US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine held an urgent Pentagon briefing on March 31, 2026, on Operation Epic Fury troop deployments and objectives including securing the Strait of Hormuz and striking nuclear sites.
Over 50,000 regional US troops plus special forces were deployed as part of Operation Epic Fury, according to March 31, 2026 Pentagon briefings.
Iran retaliated with missile and proxy attacks on Israeli energy sites on March 30, 2026, including a fire at a Haifa refinery.
The 2026 Iran war began on February 28, 2026, and reached week five by March 30, 2026.
Prior celebrations of Supreme Leader Khamenei's death in February 2026 were met with regime-organized counterprotests, indicating resilient regime control networks.
Public support in the United States for the military campaign against Iran remains low as of late March 2026.
The Iranian regime has shown resilience without major defections despite month-long escalating military strikes as of March 30, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: US military campaign has executed 13,000+ strikes across Iranian infrastructure while deploying 50,000+ regional troops under Operation Epic Fury; Iran retaliates against Israeli targets while facing leadership succession, internet blackout, infrastructure collapse, and Strait of Hormuz closure, though the regime demonstrates organizational resilience without major defections.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Pentagon briefing on March 31, 2026 detailed Operation Epic Fury objectives including 50,000+ deployed troops, Strait of Hormuz security operations, and nuclear site strikes, while IRGC commander Hassan Hassanzadeh was killed in strikes weakening Tehran security forces.
